title: Human needs
summary: 9609 AS — physical, social, esteem, and self-actualisation needs at work.
learningObjectives: Explain categories of human needs relevant to work.
Link needs to motivation and job design.
Apply needs hierarchy to case employees.

simpleExplanation.title: What people want from work
simpleExplanation.summary: Employees seek more than pay — safety, belonging, recognition, and personal growth all influence how hard they work and whether they stay.
steps: Physical: fair pay, safe conditions, rest breaks.

flashcards.back: Adequate pay, safe environment, reasonable hours, rest.
Friendship, teamwork, acceptance — Mayo's human relations emphasis.
Reaching full potential — challenging work, autonomy, learning.
Hierarchy suggests lower needs must be met before higher ones motivate (2.2.3).
Not everyone prioritises needs the same way; culture varies.
Physical/financial needs dominate — non-financial motivators insufficient alone.
Esteem and self-actualisation may matter more once pay is adequate.

sections.content: **Human needs** (2.2.2) bridges **2.2.1** and **Maslow/Herzberg (2.2.3)**. In cases, identify **which need level is unmet** — e.g. unsafe factory = physical; bullying culture = social.

sections.question: Software developers paid above market but leaving for start-ups with lower pay. Which needs might explain this?
sections.solution:
**Physical/financial:** Already satisfied — pay above market.

**Esteem & self-actualisation unmet:** Large firm bureaucracy limits **autonomy**, **interesting projects**, and **recognition**; start-ups offer **ownership**, **challenge**, and **visible impact**.

**HR response:** **Job enrichment** (2.2.4), **intrapreneurship** projects, **clear promotion paths** — address higher-order needs without only raising salary.
